app.title: InfraTrack
app.tagline: Open-source fleet intelligence for heavy machinery.

common.demoSearch: Search will be wired to the backend in a later iteration.

httpPolicy.demoNote: Active policy: GET only on the wire + POST creates when allowed (`src/app/shared/infratrack-http-policy.ts`). PUT and DELETE are not sent; matching controls are shown disabled.
httpPolicy.putDeleteUiDisabled: Server save disabled: this demo does not use PUT. See `infratrack-http-policy.ts` and set `allowPutDelete: true` when your API supports it.

profile.pageIntro: Your InfraTrack identity, operator record and session.
profile.sessionHint: Signing out clears your session and picks another preview user for this device.
profile.apiSetupTitle: Why you don’t see real MockAPI data yet
profile.apiSetupLine1: The app calls GET on your `identity` base (see `mock-api-bases.ts`). Right now that project returns HTTP 404 for the `users` and/or `operators` collections, so the UI fills in demo rows for the preview user id.
profile.apiSetupLine2: In mockapi.io create both resources with the fields you defined (id, username, email, role, …) and at least one user whose id matches the preview id in localStorage (`infratrack_preview_user_id`), or point `identity` to another project URL where those routes already work. Then reload.
profile.operatorDemoNote: User came from MockAPI, but no operator row matched this userId; showing a local placeholder until `operators` includes a matching record.
profile.offlineDemo: Preview: MockAPI did not return this user; showing local demo data for the same ID.
profile.uiIntro: Manage your account and review your plan if you are the fleet owner.
profile.errorProfile: Could not load the user profile.

subscription.sectionSubtitle: Only visible when you sign in as owner. Data comes from the API.
subscription.noData: We could not find a subscription linked to your user in the API.
subscription.noDataHint: Open “Update plan” to compare tiers; choosing one updates the record when the server allows writes (PUT).

subscriptionPlans.title: Subscription plans
subscriptionPlans.intro: Pick Basic, Premium, or Enterprise. Changes are saved with PUT when your environment allows it.
subscriptionPlans.readOnlyHint: Read-only: changing plan or auto-renew needs server write access (PUT). Ask your project admin to enable it in the HTTP policy if you use MockAPI.
subscriptionPlans.noRecord: No subscription record for your user. Create one in MockAPI or check the userId filter.
subscriptionPlans.planError: Could not save. Check MockAPI and the HTTP policy.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Use your credentials to access the InfraTrack console.
login.usernameHint: Any non-empty value is accepted in this demo.

controlPanel.title: Control Panel
controlPanel.intro: Live KPIs, telemetry on the map, Chart.js visuals, and the latest alerts with API acknowledgment.

load.error: Could not load dashboard data. Check your connection and try again.
load.empty: No data returned from the APIs (alerts / machinery).

map.title: Fleet mini-map
map.empty: No telemetry positions available to plot on the map.
map.hint: Pan and zoom the map. Click a marker for unit details. Map data © OpenStreetMap contributors.
map.dataNote: Marker positions are derived from the fleet registry for this demo (API does not include GPS coordinates).
map.dataNoteTelemetry: Positions use the latest telemetry (latitude / longitude) per unit when available; otherwise a stable fallback around Lima.

markers.warningBody: Warning · Engine temp. elevated · Site D

charts.machineryHint: Breakdown from each unit’s status field in the API.
charts.nodesHint: Online vs offline from each node’s last reported connection state.

alerts.title: Recent alerts
alerts.subtitle: Last 5 alerts (newest first)
alerts.ackError: Could not acknowledge the alert. Please try again.

maintenance.title: Recent maintenance
maintenance.empty: No maintenance records from the API.

assetManagement.title: Asset Management
assetManagement.intro: Register machinery, attach evidence, link IoT nodes and technical specs.
assetManagement.loadError: Could not load machinery from MockAPI. Check that the `machinery` resource exists on the asset-management project (see mock-api-bases.ts).
assetManagement.emptyFleet: No machines yet. Add one with the button above, or create records in MockAPI.
assetManagement.emptyFilter: No machines match the status or plate filter. Adjust your filters.

apiHint.title: Editing and deleting with MockAPI
apiHint.body: If your project has no per-item routes, the server returns 404 for PUT and DELETE on /machinery/:id. The app then saves edits and deletions in this browser’s localStorage and merges them into the table, so it can look like changes “don’t stick” compared to a raw GET from the server. Enable per-item routes on mockapi.io or use a full REST backend so everything persists server-side.
apiHint.adminDelete: The delete icon only shows when you are logged in with the administrator role.

editDialog.title: Machine record & edit
editDialog.noNodes: No nodes linked to this machine in /iotNodes.
editDialog.savedLocalOnly: MockAPI does not accept PUT /machinery/:id (404). Changes were saved in this browser (localStorage); GET /machinery will still show old data until the backend matches.
editDialog.notFound: That machine was not found in the loaded list.

deleteDialog.title: Delete machinery
deleteDialog.message: Are you sure you want to delete unit {{plate}}? This cannot be undone on the server.
deleteDialog.removedLocalOnly: DELETE by id is not available on MockAPI (404). The unit was hidden only in this browser; it may reappear on another device or if the server still returns it.
deleteDialog.error: Could not delete. Check your network or MockAPI.

addDialog.title: New machinery
addDialog.error: Could not create machinery. Check MockAPI fields and your network.

telemetry.title: GPS Map
telemetry.intro: Interactive Leaflet map from /machinery and /telemetryData; side panel with IoT status and operator assignment (admin only).

map.intro: Each marker uses the latest telemetry position per IoT node. Click a pin to open the detail panel.
map.pickMachine: Click a colored circle on the map (each one is a machine) to see plate, telemetry and IoT details here.
map.loadError: Could not load machinery, telemetry, or operators. Check your connection.
map.loadPartial: Some API requests failed. The map may be incomplete — check the browser Network tab.
map.assignError: Could not save to the server (non-404 error). Check Network or MockAPI.
map.assignLocalOnly: Your MockAPI does not expose PUT /machinery/:id (returns 404). The operator change applies only in this session; reload restores server data. Enable per-item routes on mockapi.io or use a full REST backend.
map.noNodes: No linked nodes for this machine.
map.noOperators: No operators returned from the API.

chart.title: Real-time telemetry
chart.hint: Hover points for exact values. Click legend items to show or hide each series.

reports.title: Reports & Analytics
reports.intro: Compare fleet utilization, alert mix and efficiency trends. Filters apply to the demo dataset only.

export.demoPdf: PDF export will connect to the reporting API later.
export.demoExcel: Excel export will connect to the reporting API later.

alertsCenter.title: Alert center
alertsCenter.intro: Data from GET /alerts. Filter by severity, type and acknowledgment. The toggle calls PUT /alerts/:id; if MockAPI returns 404, state is stored in this browser only.
alertsCenter.loadError: Could not load alerts. Check /alerts on the control-panel project (mock-api-bases).
alertsCenter.ownerHint: As an owner you only see alerts with critical severity.
alertsCenter.empty: No alerts match the current filters.
alertsCenter.loginToAck: Sign in to acknowledge or clear alerts.
alertsCenter.ackLocalOnly: PUT /alerts/:id is not available (404). Acknowledgment was saved in localStorage for this browser only.

performance.title: Optimization & Performance
performance.intro: KPIs derived from MockAPI (alerts, telemetry, machinery, operators), plus recent performance alerts and the operator roster.

metrics.operatorCoverageHint: Share of operators marked active in /operators.
metrics.fuelDeltaHint: Average telemetry fuel % minus a 75% reference baseline.

performance.loadError: Could not reach one or more performance APIs. Check mock bases and network.
performance.loadEmpty: All performance endpoints returned empty lists. Seed MockAPI or verify URLs.
performance.emptyAlerts: No idle / fuel / maintenance / geofence alerts to show.
performance.emptyOperators: No operators returned from the API.

editOperator.title: Edit operator
editOperator.error: Could not save. Check MockAPI and fields.

deleteOperator.title: Delete operator
deleteOperator.body: Remove {{name}} from MockAPI? This cannot be undone.
deleteOperator.error: Could not delete. Check if the operator is still linked to machinery.

configuration.title: Configuration & Support
configuration.intro: Review fleet status, manage IoT nodes (admins), and check subscriptions. Data is loaded from the server when you open this page or tap refresh.
configuration.fleetCardSubtitle: Figures based on the latest data from the server
configuration.accountTeaser: Open your profile to see role, contact data, linked operator, and sign out.

api.empty: All configuration-related endpoints returned empty data. Seed machinery, iotNodes, maintenanceRecords or subscriptions in MockAPI.

iot.cardSubtitle: Administrators only. Device status, register new hardware, and assign each node to a fleet unit.
iot.emptyNodes: No nodes yet. Add one with the button above or seed test data on the server.
iot.nonAdmin: This section is only available to administrators. Sign in with an admin account to manage IoT nodes.
iot.tableFoot: Nodes are kept for history. Saving a new assignment is only available when the environment allows server writes.
iot.linkError: Could not save the assignment. Check connectivity and environment settings.
iot.readOnlyHint: Read-only: reassigning a node to another machine needs write access on the server. It is often off in the demo; your project admin can enable it in the HTTP policy.

addIot.title: Register IoT node
addIot.machineryHint: Links the node to a machine from MockAPI.
addIot.lastSeenHint: Example: full ISO timestamp from the device.
addIot.noMachinery: No machinery returned from the API. Create at least one machine under Asset management first.
addIot.error: Could not create the IoT node. Check MockAPI and try again.

addMaint.title: Register maintenance
addMaint.nextHint: Used for “next service ≤ 30 days” in the summary.
addMaint.noMachinery: No machinery returned from the API. Create at least one machine under Asset management first.
addMaint.error: Could not create the maintenance record. Check MockAPI and try again.
